flows: accesso all'app -> 1° accesso — type=control flow; label="si"; explanation="accessing the app leads to the first access check"
accesso all'app -> 1° accesso — type=control flow; label="no"; explanation="accessing the app leads to the first access check"
1° accesso -> start — type=control flow; label="si"; explanation="if it's the first access, start the application"
1° accesso -> registrazione — type=control flow; label="no"; explanation="if it's not the first access, proceed to registration"
registrazione -> home — type=control flow; explanation="after registration, go to the home screen"
start -> utente gia' registrato? — type=control flow; explanation="after starting, check if the user is already registered"
utente gia' registrato? -> login — type=control flow; label="si"; explanation="if the user is already registered, proceed to login"
utente gia' registrato? -> registrazione — type=control flow; label="no"; explanation="if the user is not already registered, proceed to registration"
login -> home — type=control flow; explanation="after login, go to the home screen"
flow 1: accesso all'app -> 1° accesso (si) -> start -> utente gia' registrato? (si) -> login -> home
flow 2: accesso all'app -> 1° accesso (no) -> registrazione -> home
flow 3: start -> utente gia' registrato? (no) -> registrazione -> home
the diagram models the access flow of an application.
the flow starts with checking if it's the first access.
if it's the first access, the application starts, and the user is then checked for existing registration.
if the user is already registered, they proceed to login. otherwise, they are directed to registration.
after registration or successful login, the user is directed to the home screen.
home -> tragitto in corso? — type=control flow; label=""; explanation="flow to check if a route is in progress"
tragitto in corso? -> avvia tragitto — type=control flow; label="si"; explanation="if a route is in progress, start the route"
avvia tragitto -> tragitto terminato? — type=control flow; label=""; explanation="flow to check if the route is finished"
tragitto terminato? -> calcolo emissioni — type=control flow; label="si"; explanation="if the route is finished, calculate emissions"
home -> vuoi cambiare sezione? — type=control flow; label=""; explanation="flow to ask if the user wants to change section"
vuoi cambiare sezione? -> menu orizzontale — type=control flow; label="si"; explanation="if the user wants to change section, display the horizontal menu"
menu orizzontale -> home — type=control flow; label=""; explanation="return to the home screen"
tragitto terminato? -> home — type=control flow; label="no"; explanation="if the route is not finished, return to the home screen"
vuoi cambiare sezione? -> home — type=control flow; label="no"; explanation="if the user does not want to change section, return to the home screen"
flow 1: home -> tragitto in corso? (si) -> avvia tragitto -> tragitto terminato? (si) -> calcolo emissioni -> home
flow 2: home -> tragitto in corso? (no) -> home
flow 3: home -> vuoi cambiare sezione? (si) -> menu orizzontale -> home
flow 4: home -> vuoi cambiare sezione? (no) -> home
the diagram models a workflow for managing routes and calculating emissions.
the main flow involves checking if a route is in progress, starting it, checking if it's finished, and then calculating emissions.
the user can also choose to change sections, which leads to a horizontal menu and returns to the home screen.
the diagram shows two possible paths from the home node: one for route management and one for section changes.
statistiche -> visualizza report settimanale? — type=transition; label=""; explanation="flow to decision node"
visualizza report settimanale? -> report settimanale — type=transition; label="si"; explanation="transition on 'si'"
visualizza report settimanale? -> menu orizzontale — type=transition; label="no"; explanation="transition on 'no'"
report settimanale -> vuoi cambiare sezione? — type=transition; label=""; explanation="flow to decision node"
menu orizzontale -> vuoi cambiare sezione? — type=transition; label=""; explanation="flow to decision node"
vuoi cambiare sezione? -> statistiche — type=transition; label="no"; explanation="transition on 'no'"
vuoi cambiare sezione? -> menu orizzontale — type=transition; label="si"; explanation="transition on 'si'"
flow 1: statistiche -> visualizza report settimanale? (si) -> report settimanale -> vuoi cambiare sezione? -> statistiche
flow 2: statistiche -> visualizza report settimanale? (no) -> menu orizzontale -> vuoi cambiare sezione? -> menu orizzontale
the diagram models a workflow related to statistics, likely within a software application.
the user starts at the "statistiche" state and is prompted to visualize a weekly report.
based on the user's response ("si" or "no"), the flow branches to either the "report settimanale" action or the "menu orizzontale" action.
after either action, the user is asked if they want to change sections, leading back to "statistiche" if "no" or to "menu orizzontale" if "si".

4.2.5 profilo -> visualizza dati personali — type=control_flow; label=""; explanation="go to visualize personal data"

inserisci nuovi dati -> aggiorna dati — type=control_flow; label=""; explanation="update data"
vuoi aggiornare l'account? -> 4.2.5 profilo — type=control_flow; label="no"; explanation="if no, return to profile"
4.2.5 profilo -> vuoi uscite dall'account? — type=control_flow; label=""; explanation="check if user wants to logout"
vuoi uscite dall'account? -> logout — type=control_flow; label="si"; explanation="if yes, logout"
logout -> 4.2.5 profilo — type=control_flow; label=""; explanation="return to profile after logout"
4.2.5 profilo -> vuoi cambiare sezione? — type=control_flow; label=""; explanation="check if user wants to change section"
vuoi cambiare sezione? -> menu orizzontale — type=control_flow; label="si"; explanation="if yes, go to horizontal menu"
menu orizzontale -> 4.2.5 profilo — type=control_flow; label=""; explanation="return to profile after changing section"
vuoi cambiare sezione? -> 4.2.5 profilo — type=control_flow; label="no"; explanation="if no, return to profile"
flow 1: avvio app -> 4.2.5 profilo -> visualizza dati personali -> vuoi aggiornare l'account? (si) -> inserisci nuovi dati -> aggiorna dati -> 4.2.5 profilo
flow 2: avvio app -> 4.2.5 profilo -> vuoi uscite dall'account? (si) -> logout -> 4.2.5 profilo
flow 3: avvio app -> 4.2.5 profilo -> vuoi cambiare sezione? (si) -> menu orizzontale -> 4.2.5 profilo
flow 4: avvio app -> 4.2.5 profilo -> visualiz
utente -> applicazione — type=message; label="dispatch"; explanation="user sends dispatch"
applicazione -> applicazione — type=message; label="inserire credenziali"; explanation="application requests credentials"
applicazione -> server — type=message; label="invio credenziali"; explanation="application sends credentials to server"
server -> database — type=message; label="verifica dati"; explanation="server requests data verification"
database -> server — type=message; label="dati trovati"; explanation="database returns data found"
server -> applicazione — type=message; label="confronto con quelli inseriti"; explanation="server compares data"
applicazione -> utente — type=message; label="login avvenuto"; explanation="application informs user login is successful"

flow 1: utente -> applicazione (dispatch) -> applicazione (inserire credenziali) -> applicazione -> server (invio credenziali) -> server -> database (verifica dati) -> database -> server (dati trovati) -> server -> applicazione (confronto con quelli inseriti) -> applicazione -> utente (login avvenuto)
the diagram models a login sequence.
the user initiates the process by dispatching a request.
the application collects credentials and sends them to the server.
the server verifies the credentials with the database.
upon successful verification, the server informs the application, which then notifies the user that the login is successful.
the diagram does not show any error handling or alternative flows.
utente -> applicazione — type=message; label="avvio percorso"; explanation="user initiates a route"
applicazione -> server — type=message; label="calcolo e invio dati"; explanation="application sends data for calculation"
server -> database — type=message; label="aggiunta percorso"; explanation="server adds the route to the database"
database -> server — type=message; label="nuovo percorso"; explanation="database returns the new route"
server -> applicazione — type=message; label="ritorna emissioni"; explanation="server returns emissions data"
applicazione -> utente — type=message; label="emissioni co2"; explanation="application returns co2 emissions to the user"

flow 1: utente -> applicazione (avvio percorso) -> server (calcolo e invio dati) -> database (aggiunta percorso) -> server (nuovo percorso) -> applicazione (ritorna emissioni) -> utente (emissioni co2)
the diagram illustrates a sequence of interactions between a user, an application, a server, and a database to calculate and display co2 emissions for a route.
the user initiates the process by starting a route through the application.
the application then requests the server to calculate and send data.
the server adds the route to the database and retrieves the new route information.
finally, the server returns the emissions data to the application, which displays it to the user.
the flow is linear and does not include any branching or looping.

visualizza report settimanale?: si -> report settimanale
visualizza report settimanale?: no -> menu orizzontale
vuoi cambiare sezione?: si -> menu orizzontale
